name: Best-of-N
summary: N agents solve the same task independently; a judge picks the strongest answer.
about: Four agents tackle the identical prompt independently, each producing their own solution. A Judge reads all four and selects the best one, explaining its reasoning before outputting the winner. Useful when correctness matters more than cost — redundancy catches errors a single agent would miss.
example_prompts: Best programming language for a startup's first product
Is AI a net positive or negative for software developers?
Should teams prioritise TypeScript over JavaScript for new projects?
Tabs vs spaces: which side is objectively correct?

solver.description: Independently solves the task — one of several parallel attempts
solver.system: You are an expert solver. Answer the prompt as thoroughly and accurately as possible. Be direct and confident.

judge.description: Reads all solutions and selects the strongest one with reasoning
judge.system: You are a Judge. You have received multiple independent solutions to the same problem. Select the best one and explain why it is strongest. Then output the winning solution in full.
